1.name: TQQQ SMA Trend (it_3.py)
1.description: Baseline 200-day SMA filter on QQQ to manage TQQQ exposure.

2.name: Trend Vol Hybrid (it_23.py)
2.description: Combines SMA trend following with a VIX panic filter.

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ + VIX. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

3.name: Vol Ratio Trend (it_35.py)
3.description: SMA trend following with a short/long-term volatility ratio filter.

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: QQQ ≤ SMA(200) OR vol ratio ≥ 1.2 → 100% BIL

4.name: Trend Stretch Exit (it_41.py)
4.description: Trend following with mean-reversion "stretch" thresholds for exits.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: QQQ > SMA(200) AND stretch = (price - SMA)/SMA < 15% → 100% TQQQ

5.name: LETF Simple Rotation (it_101.py)
5.description: Advanced 3-of-4 regime vote (SPY, QQQ, SMH, SOXL) with overbought hedges.
5.overfit_notes: High complexity, multiple tickers and states

rules.label: Regime gate
rules.text: 3-of-4 vote: SPY, QQQ, SMH, SOXL each > SMA(202) → BULL if ≥ 3 votes

rules.label: Bull overbought (R1)
rules.text: BULL AND any RSI(15) on {SPY, QQQ, SMH, SOXL} > 72 → 100% UVIX (UVXY proxy pre-2022-03-30)

rules.label: Bull default (R3)
rules.text: BULL AND not overbought → 50% TQQQ + 50% SOXL

rules.label: Bear bounce (R4)
rules.text: BEAR AND (QQQ RSI(8) < 29 OR SMH RSI(8) < 31) → 100% SOXL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: SPY, QQQ, SMH, SOXL. Execution: TQQQ, SOXL, UVIX/UVXY

6.name: TQQQ IBS Dip (0.25/0.90, SMA200)
6.description: Internal Bar Strength (close position within high-low range) on the QQQ index triggers entry into leveraged Nasdaq exposure when IBS drops below 0.25 and the index is above its 200-day average. Exits when bars close near their highs (IBS > 0.90) or the long-term trend breaks down.
6.overfit_notes: IBS<0.25 is a standard published threshold; SMA(200) is canonical. Two tuned parameters total.

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily at -10 min before close (uses today's full bar)

7.name: TQQQ Anti-Martingale Pyramid (base 50%, +15% per 5% gain, cap 100%)
7.description: Starts at 50% TQQQ when QQQ > SMA(200). For every 5% gain above the entry price, adds another 15% allocation until reaching 100%. Liquidates on trend break. Implements the 'let winners run / cut losers' principle discussed in the 'Antifragile / 2025 best year' thread — pyramiding into strength rather than averaging into weakness.
7.overfit_notes: Anti-Martingale is a textbook position-sizing scheme; tuned step (5% gain → +15% size).

rules.label: Pyramid
rules.text: Each 5% gain above entry → +15% allocation, capped at 100%

8.name: Mega-Cap Value Averaging (cc4_007.py)
8.description: Universe-driven dip-buy on the five largest-cap U.S. stocks (selected from the top 100 by dollar volume, then ranked by market cap each universe refresh). Whenever a name pulls back more than 5% from its 20-day high it gets a 20% portfolio allocation, and the position is liquidated the moment price prints a new 20-day high. The construction sidesteps single-stock bets by spreading 100% nominal exposure across five mega-caps, and only sells into strength — never on weakness — so a position can sit in drawdown indefinitely until a fresh high releases it.
8.overfit_notes: Two tuned numbers (5% dip threshold, 20-day high lookback) and a hindsight-driven universe — 'top 5 by market cap' over 2014–2025 maps directly onto the mega-cap tech complex (AAPL/MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/META/NVDA), which is the regime the backtest favors. The rule itself (buy weakness in an uptrend, exit on new highs) is a standard value-averaging template; no exotic indicators or per-asset tuning.

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 stocks by dollar volume → top 5 by market cap (daily refresh)

rules.label: Sizing
rules.text: Fixed 20% per name (5 names → 100% gross when fully loaded)

9.name: Nasdaq-100 Breadth Rotation (cc4_010.py)
9.description: Uses participation across the 10 largest-cap U.S. stocks (drawn from the top 100 by dollar volume) as a breadth regime gate for TQQQ. Each constituent runs a 50-day EMA, and the strategy measures the fraction trading above its EMA: above 60% it goes 100% long TQQQ, below 40% it liquidates, and in the 40–60% no-man's-land it holds whatever it had. The mega-cap basket acts as a proxy for Nasdaq leadership health — when participation is broad the engine ramps full 3× exposure, and when it deteriorates it steps fully aside.
9.overfit_notes: Three tuned thresholds (50-day EMA period, 60% bull cutoff, 40% bear cutoff) and a regime-defining universe choice. The 60/40 asymmetric hysteresis is a reasonable design choice (prevents whipsaws around 50%) but the specific levels are unverified. The 'top 10 by market cap' basket suffers the same hindsight asset-selection bias as #14 — over 2014–2025 it locks in the mega-cap tech leadership that the strategy then leverages 3× into.

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 stocks by dollar volume → top 10 by market cap (signal basket)

rules.label: Indicator
rules.text: 50-day EMA per constituent; breadth = fraction trading above EMA

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: top-10 mega-cap basket. Execution: TQQQ
